summary: Frozen Quantity Consistency Fix - ALL TESTS PASSED. Catalog now correctly reads processing quantities from product_damage_history (PDH) instead of stale products.frozen_quantity and products.state. All 13 pytest tests passed. Processing filters (on_wash=39, on_laundry=7, on_restoration=4 products) and specific product quantities verified.

minor.issue: Known data issue: on_laundry=15 exceeds total=14. Code correctly handles this by showing available=0. This is a DB data issue, not a code bug.

critical_code_review_comments: PDH (product_damage_history) is now the single source of truth for processing quantities
Processing filters (on_wash/on_laundry/on_restoration) correctly query PDH instead of products.state
available_qty formula: max(0, total - reserved - in_rent - (on_wash + on_restoration + on_laundry)) - WORKING
frozen_quantity now equals sum of PDH processing quantities (on_wash + on_restoration + on_laundry)

success_rate.frontend: N/A - backend only testing per request

TX8938.note: Known data issue - on_laundry > total, available correctly capped at 0

context_for_next_testing_agent: Frozen quantity consistency fix verified. Catalog now reads from PDH (product_damage_history) as SSOT instead of products.frozen_quantity and products.state. All processing filters (on_wash, on_laundry, on_restoration) return correct counts from PDH. TX9147, TX8938, TX9150 quantities match expected values exactly.
